findings.description: Cover page typography and emphasis are not preserved.
findings.evidence: PDF cover uses bold title/subtitle and bold university name centered with strong visual hierarchy; HTML cover shows lighter-weight text and reduced emphasis, making headings less visually distinct.

findings.description: Top section layout on the first content page is loosened compared with the source.
findings.evidence: PDF page 2 has compact centered title/subtitle block above the section heading; HTML places the same elements with noticeably different spacing and a rule line, altering whitespace grouping though content remains in the same order.

findings.description: Body text formatting differs noticeably on narrative pages.
findings.evidence: Across the introductory text pages, the PDF uses more centered/narrower text blocks with stronger indentation and heading emphasis, while HTML uses wider lines and different paragraph spacing; meaning is preserved but visual distinction is reduced.

findings.description: Body text formatting differs noticeably on narrative pages.
findings.evidence: PDF maintains consistent paragraph formatting and line length; HTML uses a different measure/spacing and less pronounced paragraph indentation.

findings.description: Body text formatting differs noticeably on narrative pages.
findings.evidence: PDF paragraphs and references block have tighter, more deliberate typography; HTML renders them with different spacing and weaker visual hierarchy.

findings.description: Reading order is disrupted by a duplicated/stray reference line before Figure 1.
findings.evidence: In the PDF, the reference entry ends on the prior page and Figure 1 starts cleanly; in the HTML, the continuation line 'Project and North Carolina State Parks...' appears above the Figure 1 image, so a reader encounters figure content after an out-of-place reference fragment.

findings.description: A stray figure label appears below the species distribution figure.
findings.evidence: PDF page shows Figure 3 caption cleanly beneath the map; HTML includes an extra standalone 'A' below the caption, creating an unnecessary extra element in the layout.

findings.description: A stray page/year marker appears around Figure 4 content.
findings.evidence: PDF presents Figure 4 with caption and note only; HTML includes extra standalone text '2021' and 'A' below the note, altering page structure.

findings.description: Figure 5 map content is incorrect/incomplete in one HTML rendering.
findings.evidence: PDF Figure 5 shows many yellow occurrence markers around the lake for Tuckerman’s Pondweed; the HTML counterpart shown for that page contains only a single marker near the top-left while keeping the same caption indicating 19 sample sites, which materially changes the figure information.

findings.description: Figure 6 map content does not match the source figure.
findings.evidence: PDF Figure 6 shows a single yellow marker for Variable Pondweed at 1 sample site in 2021; the HTML counterpart displays multiple markers distributed around the lake, conflicting with the caption and the source image.

findings.description: A stray figure label appears before Figure 10 continuation.
findings.evidence: PDF transitions directly between figures; HTML includes an isolated black box/marker above the Figure 10 caption area, which is not part of the source layout.

findings.description: An extra standalone '2020' text appears between figures.
findings.evidence: PDF moves from Figure 10 to Figure 11 without an isolated year label; HTML inserts standalone '2020' text between figures, disrupting layout grouping.

findings.description: Heading hierarchy is not accurate to the document structure. The document title is split into h1 and h2, and the 'References:' label is promoted to a heading level that does not clearly match the PDF structure.
findings.evidence:
HTML uses `

2021 White Lake

` and `

Aquatic Vegetation Survey

`, while the PDF title page presents both lines as centered title text without a clear section hierarchy. HTML also uses `

References:

` after the main body, while the PDF shows 'References:' as a bold label near the bottom of the page rather than a clearly nested heading.

findings.description: Some figure content is duplicated or echoed in HTML with stray standalone labels not functioning as captions.
findings.evidence:
HTML includes extra text nodes such as `

A

` before Figures 4, 5, and 10, and `

2020

` before Figure 11, plus `

2021

` after Figure 4. In the PDF, these are panel labels or map annotations embedded within figures, not separate body text elements. HTML also nests `

findings.description: There are several text inaccuracies and OCR/conversion errors that alter wording or introduce grammatical mistakes.
findings.evidence: PDF title page reads 'Survey Conducted by:' and 'North Carolina State University'; HTML matches this, but body text has errors such as `It’s population has been present at 14 - 34%` instead of the PDF's possessive form 'Its population...'. HTML says 'Continued monitoring and management of hydrilla should be remain a priority.' which is grammatically corrupted versus the PDF sentence 'Continued monitoring and management of hydrilla should remain a priority.' Figure 5 caption in HTML says '19 samples site in 2021' while the PDF reads '19 sample sites in 2021'.

findings.description: Some alt text is generic or slightly inaccurate relative to the figure content.
findings.evidence: HTML includes alt text like 'Decorative graphic or legend label' for a figure image fragment and 'Map showing Macroalgae at 54 sample sites in 2021' where the PDF caption specifies 'Macroalgae (Nitella spp.)'. Most alt text is helpful, but a few entries are generic or omit scientific naming shown in the PDF.
